data.title: SAP’s Growing Defense Role Reshapes AI And Revenue Risk Profile
data.description:
• SAP (XTRA:SAP) is seeing rapid growth in its defense sector business, now described by the CEO as the company’s fastest growing revenue stream.
  • The expansion is tied to rising global demand for military software and AI, as governments increase spending on digital defense capabilities.
  • This defense focus marks a new chapter for SAP, extending its role from enterprise and cloud software into supporting military operations and infrastructure.

At a current share price of €153.82, SAP sits at an interesting crossroads between its established enterprise software base and a growing presence in defense. Recent returns show a mixed picture, with the stock up 40.2% over 3 years and 62.3% over 5 years, but with a 1 year decline of 38.4% and a year to date decline of 23.8%. Over shorter periods, the shares are down 7.6% over 7 days and 11.3% over 30 days. These shorter-term moves may matter for readers watching entry timing closely.

The rise of defense related software and AI work introduces a fresh angle to the SAP story that goes beyond the usual focus on cloud and ecosystem partnerships. Investors watching XTRA:SAP may want to track how deeply this segment integrates into the core business, how it affects revenue mix, and how management frames any new risks or constraints tied to serving military clients. Over time, the scale and stability of this defense exposure could become a key factor in how the company is perceived and valued by the market.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for SAP by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on SAP.

We've flagged 0 risks for SAP. See which could impact your investment.

SAP’s defense exposure, now at roughly 10% of group revenue, plugs directly into the company’s broader push to make AI and cloud software core to national infrastructure. Defense customers tend to buy long-term, mission-critical systems, which can support high switching costs and deep integration with SAP’s existing ERP and data platforms. At the same time, this shifts part of SAP’s growth story into a more politically sensitive and highly regulated area, where contracts can be large but also subject to budget cycles, export controls, and scrutiny over AI use in military settings. For investors, the key question is whether this new revenue stream stays tightly linked to SAP’s core strengths in data management and AI governance, such as the LatticeFlow AI partnership, or starts pulling the company into areas where execution and reputational risks are harder to control.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The SAP Narrative

  • The defense push supports the existing narrative that SAP aims to deepen its role as a system-of-record, by extending cloud and Business AI into high-stakes, long-contract environments that can reinforce recurring revenue.
  • It also challenges the narrative’s focus on implementation complexity in commercial sectors, because defense projects like the German armed forces migration can introduce different technical hurdles, approval processes, and potential delays.
  • The current narrative emphasises digitalisation, partnerships, and AI agents, but does not fully account for how a 10% defense revenue mix could change SAP’s risk profile, regulatory exposure, or customer concentration.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for SAP to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Higher geopolitical and regulatory risk as defense work expands, which could affect contracts, public perception, and compliance costs if AI is used in sensitive military applications.
  • ⚠️ Execution risk on complex defense deployments, where technical issues or delays in projects like large-scale armed forces migrations could impact margins or client relationships.
  • 🎁 Exposure to defense software and AI spend, which can involve large, multi-year contracts that fit well with SAP’s focus on recurring, high-value enterprise relationships.
  • 🎁 Potential for tighter integration of AI-governance tools, such as the LatticeFlow AI partnership, into defense and public-sector workflows, reinforcing SAP’s positioning as a compliance-ready platform provider.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors may want to watch how quickly defense revenue grows from the current 10% level, whether SAP discloses more about margins on these contracts, and how often defense wins are tied to its core cloud and Business AI offerings rather than one off projects. It is also worth tracking progress and any reported issues on deployments with national clients, along with how SAP addresses AI governance, especially in light of frameworks such as the EU AI Act, where platforms like LatticeFlow AI play a role.

data.title: Is Freshworks (FRSH) Now An Opportunity After A 48% One-Year Share Price Slide
data.description:
• Wondering whether Freshworks is starting to look like value or a value trap? This article breaks down what the current share price might really be offering you.
  • The stock last closed at US$8.03, with returns of 8.2% over 30 days but a 48.3% decline over the past year that may have changed how investors view its risk and reward profile.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Freshworks as a mid cap software name that is still trying to win investor confidence after a 30.8% year to date decline and a 42.0% drop over three years. Commentators have been asking whether sentiment has simply swung too far given the business model and sector.
  • On Simply Wall St's 6 point valuation checklist, Freshworks has a valuation score of 4. This sets up a closer look at how different valuation methods compare and hints at an even more useful way to think about value at the end of this article.

Find out why Freshworks's -48.3% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Freshworks Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model projects the cash a company is expected to generate in the future and then discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business might be worth now.

For Freshworks, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach built on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is reported at about $225.1 million. Analyst inputs extend out a few years, with Simply Wall St extrapolating further to estimate Free Cash Flow of $493.4 million by 2035, based on a path that runs through $249.5 million in 2026 and $326.9 million in 2028.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $23.99 per share. At the recent share price of $8.03, this implies a discount of roughly 66.5%. This indicates that the market price is well below the model’s estimate of value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Freshworks is undervalued by 66.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 52 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Freshworks.

Approach 2: Freshworks Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings, which is often how the market frames value in mature or consistently profitable names.

What counts as a “normal” P/E really depends on how fast earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings look. Higher growth or more predictable earnings can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher perceived risk usually points to a lower one.

Freshworks currently trades on a P/E of 12.42x. That sits below the Software industry average P/E of 29.37x and well below the peer group average of 61.84x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Freshworks is 3.95x. This is the P/E level it estimates could be appropriate after factoring in elements like earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and key risks.

The Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for company specific characteristics rather than assuming one size fits all. Comparing 12.42x with the Fair Ratio of 3.95x, the current valuation comes out higher than that model suggests.

Result: OVERVALUED

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider CACI International (CACI) After Its Strong Multi‑Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether CACI International's share price still offers value after a strong run, this breakdown will help you frame that question using several valuation lenses.
  • The stock last closed at US$606.38, with returns of 6.9% over 30 days, 12.8% year to date, 65.6% over 1 year, 110.8% over 3 years, and 151.0% over 5 years. This naturally raises questions about what is already reflected in the price.
  • Recent news coverage has focused on CACI International in the context of its share price performance and investor interest, giving investors more reasons to look closely at what is built into expectations today. This article was prompted to provide an evergreen look at the company so you can assess valuation beyond short term headlines.
  • CACI International currently has a valuation score of 3/6, meaning it screens as undervalued on half of the checks used. The next sections will walk through those traditional methods before circling back to a fuller way of thinking about value at the end of the article.

CACI International delivered 65.6% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Professional Services industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: CACI International Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today, so you can compare that value with the current share price.

For CACI International, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow stands at about $637.3 million. Analyst and extrapolated estimates point to free cash flow of around $731.3 million in 2026 and $831.2 million by 2028, with Simply Wall St extending those projections further out to 2035 using its own assumptions.

Aggregating and discounting these projected cash flows gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $817.05 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $606.38, the model implies the stock trades at roughly a 25.8% discount to this estimate. Under this approach, the shares screen as undervalued.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests CACI International is undervalued by 25.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 52 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for CACI International.

Approach 2: CACI International Price vs Earnings

P/E is a useful way to look at profitable companies because it ties what you pay directly to the earnings they generate. A higher P/E can reflect stronger expected growth or lower perceived risk, while a lower P/E can point to more modest growth expectations or higher uncertainty.

CACI International currently trades on a P/E of about 25.8x. That sits above the Professional Services industry average of roughly 19.1x, but below the peer group average of around 31.4x. On the surface, that suggests the market prices CACI International at a premium to the broader industry, yet not as highly as some peers.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for CACI International is 24.7x. This is a proprietary estimate of what a reasonable P/E could be for the company, taking into account factors such as its earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk characteristics. Because it adjusts for these company specific traits, the Fair Ratio can give a more tailored view than simple comparisons to peers or industry averages. With the current P/E only slightly above the Fair Ratio, the shares appear to be roughly fairly valued on this measure.

Approach 1: Coterra Energy Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today’s value using a required rate of return.

For Coterra Energy, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, built on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow (FCF) is about $1.58b. Analyst based forecasts extend out to 2030, with projected FCF in that year of about $3.08b, and Simply Wall St extrapolates further annual figures to complete the model.

Based on these cash flows, the DCF model output suggests an estimated intrinsic value of about $91.68 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $33.97, this implies the stock is trading at a 62.9% discount on this set of assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Coterra Energy Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings, because it links the share price directly to the business’s current profit generation.

Growth expectations and risk both influence what feels like a normal or fair P/E. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk often goes with a lower P/E.

Coterra’s current P/E is 15.03x, compared with an Oil and Gas industry average of 15.81x and a peer group average of 14.60x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Coterra is 21.37x, which is a proprietary estimate of the P/E that might be expected given factors such as the company’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio goes further than simple peer or industry comparisons, because it adjusts for the mix of growth, risk, profitability, sector and size, rather than relying only on broad group averages that may not match Coterra’s characteristics closely.

Since the Fair Ratio of 21.37x is above the current P/E of 15.03x, this framework indicates that Coterra is trading below that implied level.
